content.text: Select True or False for each question:

questions.text: Greek nouns are either singular or plural.
The nominative case is the subject of a sentence. It is the doer of the verbal action.
The accusative case is for something directly affected by the verbal action. For example, in the sentence, ‘Al gave John a gift’, the word ‘gift’ is directly affected by the verbal action.

Many Greek feminine nouns following the regular first declension pattern have hybrid endings if their last consonant is a ς or ξ. They will have case endings in alpha in the singular and eta in the plural.
Greek feminine nouns following the first declension pattern with case endings in alpha have ambiguous genitive singular and accusative plural forms, when not accompanied by the article.
The dative case is the direct object of a sentence.
